commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3a7d15383c6ffa833c4cfa169899cea126218228)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds canonical `SessionEntry.route` metadata and shared route projection helpers, derives legacy delivery fields from routes, updates ACP/subagent/session delivery paths, deprecates ad hoc subagent delivery-origin hook fields, and adds tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the PR as a refactor; the concrete review finding is visible directly in the diff because the added docs page says it is temporary and should be deleted before merge.
commands.review_followup: The protected `maintainer` label plus broad compatibility/message-delivery/session-state risks make this an owner-review item; the only mechanical cleanup is deleting the temporary docs page.

target.title: refactor(channels): unify session route projection
target.body:
## Summary

This refactor makes channel delivery route metadata a core-owned projection instead of a set of ad hoc plugin hints.

- Adds `ChannelRouteRef` as canonical optional session metadata via `SessionEntry.route`.
- Adds `src/channels/route-projection.ts` for shared route projection across delivery contexts, sessions, conversation refs, and session binding records.
- Updates session load/update paths to normalize route metadata first, then project compatibility fields (`deliveryContext`, `lastChannel`, `lastTo`, `lastAccountId`, `lastThreadId`).
- Preserves provider-specific route metadata such as `rawTo`, `chatType`, `thread.kind`, and `thread.source` while still writing legacy mirrors for current callers.
- Moves ACP spawn and subagent announce delivery onto shared binding-to-route projection instead of duplicating route construction.
- Updates chat send origin resolution, agent session updates, and the sessions list tool to read route-aware delivery context.
- Adds stale-route cleanup so non-thread system events and legacy main-route retirement do not resurrect old thread IDs.
- Adds malformed persisted route guards so old or externally edited session stores cannot crash normalization.

## Why

The Discord subagent thread issue was not fundamentally Discord-specific. Discord exposed it first because child sessions can be bound to a newly created thread, but core then needed a separate optional plugin hook result (`deliveryOrigin`) to know where to route follow-up delivery. TypeScript could not protect the missing value because that hook result was intentionally optional and core treated it as a best-effort hint.

This moves the reusable part into core: once core has a binding/conversation identity, it can project that identity into canonical route metadata, then derive compatibility delivery fields from that single route. Provider-specific creation and target resolution still live in plugins, but core owns the normalized route shape it stores and compares.

## Route Model After This PR

- `ChannelRouteRef`: canonical route metadata stored on sessions and used by core projection helpers.
- `ChannelOutboundSessionRoute`: still the channel adapter's executable route for actual outbound delivery.
- `ConversationRef`: still binding identity, not a send target by itself.
- `DeliveryContext`: compatibility projection for protocol, tools, sentinels, and older session code.
- Legacy session fields: retained as mirrors for compatibility, now derived from the canonical route where possible.

## Deprecations

Deprecated plugin hook outputs/APIs:

- `PluginHookSubagentSpawningResult.threadBindingReady`
- `PluginHookSubagentSpawningResult.deliveryOrigin`
- `PluginHookSubagentDeliveryTargetResult`
- `subagent_delivery_target` hook result shape through the deprecated result type

These remain available for SDK compatibility during the transition. Bundled/core paths now prefer canonical route projection where the binding/conversation contract has enough information.

Not deprecated in this PR:

- `DeliveryContext`, because protocol/tool/session compatibility still depends on it.
- `lastChannel`, `lastTo`, `lastAccountId`, and `lastThreadId`, because persisted sessions and external tooling still read them.
- `ChannelOutboundSessionRoute`, because adapters still own executable outbound routing.
- `ConversationRef`, because bindings still need identity separate from delivery.

## Compatibility

Existing sessions continue to load:

- Old entries without `route` hydrate from `deliveryContext` and legacy last-route fields.
- New writes persist both `route` and legacy mirrors.
- `route` wins when present, but route metadata is merged with compatibility delivery context instead of being dropped.
- Non-object persisted `route` values normalize to `undefined` instead of throwing.
- Non-thread system events clear canonical route threads as well as legacy `lastThreadId`/`deliveryContext.threadId`.
- Legacy main-route retirement clears canonical `route` alongside compatibility fields.

## Provider/Thread Notes

Slack, Discord, Feishu, Matrix, Telegram, Mattermost, and other plugins can keep provider-specific thread and target semantics inside their channel/binding adapters. Core does not infer child-thread support merely because a provider has some thread concept. MS Teams therefore remains on normal thread send support unless/until durable final thread binding semantics are explicitly exposed through the same generic route/binding contracts.

## Temporary Refactor Doc

`docs/refactor/channels.md` is included in the first PR push as the implementation checklist requested for this refactor. After the PR is green and this PR description is verified to capture the complete plan, I will delete that file before merge so the temporary plan does not ship in docs.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: canonical channel route metadata is stored on sessions, used to derive compatibility delivery fields, and shared across ACP/subagent route projection.

Real environment tested: local checkout plus Testbox changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.agent.subagent-delivery-context.test.ts src/channels/route-projection.test.ts src/utils/delivery-context.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.session-key-normalization.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.thread-binding.test.ts`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --mode testbox --label channel-route-unification-check --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:

- Focused Vitest proof passed: 6 shards, 176 tests.
- Testbox changed gate passed: `tbx_01krxr1nm294c131fzrg8d07ej`.
- GitHub Actions proof for Testbox: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26040095900
- Autoreview accepted three earlier findings, all fixed in follow-up commits:
  - preserve route metadata on agent updates;
  - guard malformed persisted route values;
  - clear stale canonical route threads for non-thread system events and legacy main-route retirement.
- Final autoreview reported no remaining code findings; only the expected temporary-doc cleanup before merge.

Observed result after fix: route-aware session updates preserve canonical route metadata, route projection tests cover binding/session/delivery-context conversion, and changed gate passed.

What was not tested: live provider delivery against every external chat network; this PR uses unit/integration coverage plus Testbox changed gate for the refactor surface.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
